lowerLayerChecks.evidence: 3/3 split packets have literal edge persistence

theoremCandidate.statement: The p=13 lane has 3/3 split profiles assembled into theorem-facing readiness candidates.

openAllNHandoffGaps.note: Prove every future p=13 threatening row lands in one of the emitted split profiles, or emit the missing split profile as a new atom.
Replace each sampled K-envelope comparison with the symbolic K(N,x) inequality for the full p=13 lane.
If future rows require moving vertex formulas, re-lift vertex presence, edge persistence, and disjointness for the parameterized families.

firstPrimeLaneHandoffCandidate.proofBoundary: This aggregates split-discharge readiness for a prime lane. It is not a universal D-lane proof until row-universe coverage and the symbolic K(N,x) lift are discharged.
firstPrimeLaneHandoffCandidate.nextTheoremAction: Discharge row_universe_split_coverage first, then universal_k_formula_lift; do not mark the parent lane done until those all-N gaps close.
